idea: Treat human correction latency as a physical budget, quantify how often corrections that exceed the staleness limit cause takeover failures, and use consequence-adaptive slowing to keep corrections within the human reaction-time budget.
reason: overlap=medium with no single-paper headline hit. The scripted-delayed-human falsification experiment is executable, but the core—human reaction time vs critical/irreversible deadlines as a time budget—is shared with the driving takeover-time-budget line (2408.15682/ISO 21959). Mechanism transfer creates a nonzero target-domain hit→SA is capped at AwR.

idea: Treat open-loop action chunks as write-back caching and show that, under partial observability, silent consistency loss is fundamentally invisible to observation-based triggers; quantify its share of chunk failures and bound the ceiling of "better replanning trigger" approaches.
reason: overlap=low with no single-paper hit. The falsification experiment (sim privileged truth separating silent/observable cases + measuring the strongest observation trigger's recall ceiling on the silent set) is executable and decisive, but the formal core=DES diagnosability is a mature mechanism transfer, and priorwork explicitly judges the difference "insufficient" for clear-accept→novelty is capped at AwR.

idea: Remove the requirement that a policy use one output representation throughout execution; switch representations online by task phase, using coarse temporal commitments in free space and dense refinement during contact, so representation choice becomes a state-conditioned decision rather than a global design choice.
reason: Prescreen direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.17237

idea: Replace pixel/latent prediction with a headline objective of a "reachable-goal-set contraction field": after every state-action transition, estimate how much of the demonstrated goal set remains reachable, yielding a world model that directly outputs irreversibility/commitment for safe replanning gates.
reason: Reachable-set contraction as a primary WM objective+MPC gating is not covered by one paper and the falsification experiment has a decisive attribution arm, but RAE/RAC already occupy "self-supervised irreversibility signal→gating," and the difference is insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty. Only 1 paper was fully read; high-source-similarity neighbors (Reachability-Weighted-Resampling/SAGE) were checked only from search snippets, making the prior-work review weak (MAJOR), so it stops at accept-w-rev.

idea: Treat numerical precision at every denoising step×chunk-horizon position in a K-step flow-matching action head as an unallocated compute control; use oracle nonuniform precision scheduling to test whether fixed precision wastes compute during contact, with contact-concentrated precision sensitivity as the signal.
reason: CRITICAL (F1): the headline "per-denoising-step precision is an unallocated control" is directly falsified by DiffPro (stepwise precision+budget constraints), while contact-phase compute concentration is occupied in robot closed loop by D3P; with overlap medium-high, the residual is only the thin conjunction "precision axis×horizon position×contact phase," a confirmatory increment dominated by the union D3P+DiffPro and unable to establish a clear-accept-level difference within the lifecycle.

idea: Predict world-model dynamics in an end-effector/contact-centered reference frame that explicitly cancels embodiment self-motion rather than in world coordinates; at matched parameter count, test whether contact-frame invariance reduces long-horizon rollout drift, with gains disappearing on noncontact tasks as attribution.
reason: Contact-frame prediction+explicit ego cancellation has a matched-parameter attribution arm, a "noncontact gain disappears" attribution, and a decisive feasible falsification experiment. Equivariant-WM authors explicitly state "invariance≠lower error," leaving a gap for reduced drift (3 papers fully read, no domination). However, equivariant WMs occupy the causal link "frame invariance→stable per-step error," and priorwork judges the increment likely insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty at accept-w-rev.

idea: Teleoperation latency is part of the demonstration policy, not collection noise: BC learns phase-shifted actions after latency compensation, causing systematic anticipation or overdamping in low-latency deployment; deconvolving latency from demonstrations should recover contact-phase performance.
reason: Prescreen direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08776

idea: Remove the load-bearing requirement that cross-robot co-training project every trajectory into unified low-level action coordinates; instead co-train local latent-action charts induced by observation transitions, then learn the chart-to-control readout from a small target-embodiment sample.
reason: Prescreen direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.18955

idea: Successful-demonstration corpora systematically censor abandonment and failure segments, hiding unrecoverable boundaries from the policy; use pre-failure windows as do-not-clone negative samples to reduce irreversible contact errors at the same number of successful demonstrations.
reason: CRITICAL F1: priorwork has a direct hit in offline imitation using expert/undesirable/negative demonstrations; the difference, "pre-failure windows as do-not-clone negative samples," is insufficient for a new paper.

idea: The main distortion in manipulation world models is not unrealistic future imagery, but plausible-looking contact consequences for actions outside the support; explicit action-support calibration could remove OOD optimism from offline policy ranking.
reason: CRITICAL F1: WorldGym reports OOD action overestimation, and WAV formalizes action reachability/support; this idea's headline estimand strongly overlaps the nearest priorwork.

idea: Some negative transfer in cross-embodiment co-training comes from unrecorded control-stiffness/compliance differences rather than geometry; estimating and conditioning on compliance as a hidden domain variable could isolate contact-phase transfer failures.
reason: There is no single-paper direct hit, but RMA latent-variable adaptation, variable impedance learning, and cross-embodiment work constrain the idea from three directions. Novelty reaches only medium and must narrow to the first compliance-as-hidden-domain-variable demonstration.

idea: Remote supervision is not one intervention button but a bandwidth-limited sensing channel: the robot actively selects low-bitrate views, state summaries, and pause postures so one supervisor makes fewer mistaken interventions in the same time.
reason: Prior-work review finds close neighbors for active view selection, operator preference, and ask-for-help. The difference narrows to equal-bandwidth information orchestration within HIL help events, enough for a bounded problem but insufficient for clear-accept/SA.

idea: Errors in contact-rich world models arise primarily from which contact relations must be remembered rather than pixel-prediction error; explicitly maintain an action-modifiable contact causal working set and use working-set churn to predict long-horizon failure.
reason: Contact-rich world models and contact-evolution work are densely populated. The contact causal working set/churn is a measurable new estimand, but its difference from ContactWorld/tactile WM still requires experimental support.

idea: Cross-embodiment action labels should not first be projected into unified coordinates; decompose them into constraints executable by the target embodiment and source-private execution details, placing non-executable details in the distillation residual rather than the supervision target.
reason: CRITICAL F1: priorwork on latent actions, embodiment-specific decoders, and cross-embodiment action extraction covers the main structure at high overlap; the residual interpretation is insufficient for a clear-accept difference.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that embodied evaluation and training require an external resetter: turn tasks into reset-free continuous workflows, replace manual episode resets with learned self-reset/cleanup actions, and test whether VLA leaderboard capability is merely outsourced reset capability.
reason: reset-free/VLA benchmark confound is distinct but priorwork shows medium overlap and closest reset-free RL/VLM-finetuning work weakens clear-accept novelty; missing RoboFuME-style baseline also remains major.

idea: Current robustness evaluation conflates disturbance difficulty with error reversibility: the same success-rate gap may only reflect different probabilities of entering unrecoverable basins; match recoverability before evaluation to measure actual recovery ability.
reason: recoverability-margin matching is a plausible estimand repair with executable falsification, but priorwork already covers recoverable-state values and recovery-aware metrics, so novelty is not clear-accept yet.

idea: A VLA language tower may not carry continuous semantic reasoning in closed-loop control, but mainly an implicit termination contract; compile instructions into verifiable termination/forbidden-region contracts for a small control head, then test whether false completion comes from missing contracts rather than weak visual grounding.
reason: false-completion contract is feasible but heavily surrounded by ReViP/progress/predicate-monitor work, and the proposed false-completion signal does not prove the stronger claim that language towers mainly carry termination contracts.

idea: Evolution: cross-embodiment co-training resamples heterogeneous control frequencies onto one time base, systematically aliasing high-frequency contact transitions; after measuring alias prevalence in real corpora, narrow the negative-transfer attribution to a data-pipeline artifact.
reason: Aliasing attribution in real corpora is falsifiable and useful, but close work such as SPACE already covers control-frequency variation; the current difference is insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Human-in-the-loop systems treat "no intervention" as a safety label, but silence often reflects occupied supervisor attention; model nonresponse as censoring to avoid treating unattended dangerous states as negative samples.
reason: No intervention as an attention-dependent-censoring estimand is valuable, but a scripted supervisor creates a measurement-claim mismatch when validating human silence labels.

idea: Closed-loop VLA failures are governed by contact-phase tail latency, not mean inference latency; latency distributions with the same mean but different tails should produce a predictable crossing in failure rates.
reason: The contact-phase tail-latency proposition is clear and inexpensive to test, but VLA latency/action-aware scheduling has dense neighbors; without SP-VLA/ReactVLA-level baselines, the idea cannot be upgraded.

idea: Evolution: compute-adaptive safety margins—when shared or edge compute is cut, a budget-conditioned policy automatically widens spatial margins and slows down, trading speed for safety under compute starvation instead of collapsing through passive degradation.
reason: The mechanism core, "conditioning inference latency/compute into the policy during training," is occupied by 2512.05964; safety-margin/speed modulation is a real difference, but independent prior-work review explicitly finds it insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty (MAJOR×1). The minimal falsification experiment is executable and decisive, raising it only to AwR.

idea: The primary cause of instruction-following loss during VLA fine-tuning is not erased semantic knowledge but damaged language→vision-action attention routing: knowledge probes remain intact, and repairing only routing can recover instruction following without web co-training.
reason: overlap=high: the headline causal account, "alignment/routing breaks rather than knowledge," was named by ChatVLA; "routing is the lesion+repairing routing alone cures it" was published by IGAR on the same model family, and the repair arm is occupied. Only the half-sentence "probes remain intact" remains, so the F1 case is CRITICAL and directly rejects the idea (the minimal falsification experiment also omits IGAR as the strongest baseline, MAJOR×1).

idea: BC/VLA validation loss fails to select good checkpoints because it penalizes legitimate branch choice together with within-branch execution error, not because of evaluation noise or composite error; isolating within-branch error should correlate strongly with closed-loop success and provide a rollout-free selector.
reason: The branch-mixture attribution axis is not occupied by a single paper, but the same problem and deliverable (a decomposed rollout-free selector) are occupied by CI-MSE (2606.29898), and the difference is "insufficiently certain" (MAJOR). The minimal falsification experiment also omits CI-MSE as a baseline, so it can "succeed" while remaining dominated (MAJOR); ≥2 MAJOR findings cap it at accept-w-rev.

idea: Test whether physical-conservation residuals in a world model predict downstream model-based policy failures better than pixel/latent prediction errors, making physical fidelity a better WM selection signal.
reason: After recheck, overlap=medium: each half of the headline is covered by existing work (2002.04523/2605.06388 and 2606.22363), and prior-work review judges the combined difference insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty. A selection-only diagnostic has no 8+ dimension and a borderline ceiling.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that robot data engines must archive raw pixels for future retraining: store only frozen-backbone representations+actions+proprioceptive streams and a 1% keyframe sample, then test under an equal storage budget whether pixels are truly load-bearing.
reason: Patent US11475291B2 occupies all three conceptual elements+the "future retraining" experiment cannot measure backbone evolution, the main setting where pixels could be load-bearing (estimand mismatch)+DROID success-rate evaluation is infeasible for one researcher on 1×H100, yielding 3 MAJOR findings; the assumption-removal channel closes because conditions ① and ④ fail.

idea: Physical distortion in video world models is not caused by a pixel-prediction objective lacking physical constraints; tokenizer rate-distortion discards low-salience, load-bearing evidence such as contact/support at the encoder. At equal bitrate, a contact-weighted tokenizer should repair physical errors better than a larger transformer.
reason: The encoding-side causal account is jointly occupied by DA-VAE in imagery and Phaedra in science, and prior-work review judges the specialized difference insufficient for clear-accept, capping novelty below SA. The discriminating design is clean, the repair arm is bound to the claim, and execution is feasible on 1×H100, yielding borderline value.

idea: Decompose coupled scene dynamics in a world model into an action-write cone and an exogenous cone, supervising them through action permutation so the planner cannot mistake autonomously moving objects for controllable ones.
reason: Prescreen direct hit: https://openreview.net/forum?id=6VnyYkg37h

idea: Unify the action generator and external safety filter into one barrier-score flow: the tangential score generates task actions, while the normal score simultaneously provides the safety boundary and pushback direction, so safety correction is not a second post-generation system.
reason: CRITICAL: SafeFlow already covers the headline of placing the barrier inside flow generation dynamics and correcting along the full trajectory; priorwork judges overlap=high, and the residual learned signed field plus tangential-normal parameterization is insufficient for clear accept.

idea: Decompose continuous actions into reversible on-manifold corrections and commitment components that cross irreversible boundaries; the VLA explicitly decides only the latter at low frequency, exposing when an action truly becomes irreversible.
reason: Recoverability-based action coordinates and a commitment token retain some residual mechanism novelty, and the experiment can kill the claim; however, action-level recoverability and adaptive commitment have direct precursors, which the current baselines do not cover.

idea: Unify active observation with action chunking as a conditionally branching chunk: a shared prefix first creates information, an observation predicate selects among execution suffixes, and one generation expresses "look first, then act on the result."
reason: A shared probing prefix, explicit predicate, and conditional suffix form a testable structure, but DREAM-Chunk and CoMe-VLA respectively cover multiple-future selection and active-perception branches; the minimal experiment also omits both as direct baselines.

idea: Interpret a human's physical push/pull correction as a local constraint gradient rather than an action segment to imitate: one force correction directly creates a reusable state-conditioned half-space that constrains later similar actions.
reason: Safe MPC Alignment directly covers the headline of physical directional correction as first-order safety-constraint information forming a half-space update, creating a novelty CRITICAL.

idea: Turn irreversible manipulation into an "action with a physical receipt": before execution, the VLA predicts the force/tactile witness that should appear; it commits the action suffix only if the witness arrives on time, otherwise withdrawing while contact remains reversible.
reason: No single close work fully covers explicitly committing an irreversible suffix only after a physical witness arrives, but predicted touch, feedback control, and dynamic commitment all have strong neighbors; a clear-accept-level difference is unverified.

idea: Have the robot emit an imperceptible mechanical chirp and use its contact-microphone echo to identify local stiffness and looseness online, giving the world model an action-conditioned material query before applying real force.
reason: VibeCheck and related work directly cover active swept-frequency excitation, contact-acoustic hidden-state identification, and integration into a transition model and manipulation policy, creating a novelty CRITICAL.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that multi-view VLAs require pre-calibrated, fixed camera extrinsics; use online cross-view point-map consistency as the only shared coordinates so arbitrarily placed or moving cameras can join the policy directly.
reason: GP3 covers the headline of multi-view geometry with unknown camera parameters entering a manipulation policy directly; the remaining point-map alignment and hot-plugging differences cannot pass the novelty gate.

idea: Let an object-centric world model recompute only tokens reached by propagating physical-constraint residuals: freeze regions whose contact, support, or rigid-body consistency is satisfied, and move compute dynamically along the constraint-propagation frontier.
reason: Physical-residual-frontier scheduling has a nontrivial low-overlap difference and an executable falsification experiment, but it lacks strong learned router/Mixture-of-Depths baselines; the expected 35% FLOPs savings are also insufficient for a clear-accept/oral ceiling.

idea: Extract partial-order graphs of object-relation topology events—separation, contact, support, containment—from videos without action labels, and pretrain a VLA's long-horizon planning representation on which events commute and which require precedence.
reason: The 2019 precedence graph occupies the headline that video supplies a partial order of commutative events, and ALAM occupies action-free temporal-composition-to-VLA. The current experiment omits both as strong baselines, leaving insufficient integration novelty for clear accept.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that action generation requires a generative distribution covering multiple modes; test whether a unimodal iterative policy matches diffusion on genuinely multimodal tasks, directly testing whether multimodal coverage is load-bearing.
reason: The load-bearing-assumption form and minimal falsification experiment are cheap and decisive, but the headline has medium overlap with MARS (selective multimodality), and priorwork judges the difference insufficient for clear-accept. SA exception condition ①, zero headline hits, fails (overlap=medium)→novelty is capped.

idea: Cross-embodiment negative transfer may arise mainly because each robot dataset binds one embodiment to a fixed camera placement, confounding viewpoint distribution with embodiment dynamics; balancing or randomizing viewpoints should remove most negative transfer.
reason: Viewpoint dependence is established, and RoVi-Aug already augments camera placement. Although the causal attribution has no single-paper hit, overlap=medium and priorwork judges the difference insufficient for clear-accept→novelty is capped; the experiment is clear, decisive, and includes a repair arm, so it remains borderline.

idea: Long-horizon rollout drift in learned world models may not be compounded stepwise error, but step changes caused by averaging bimodal futures at contact-formation/breakage regime transitions; the error-growth curve should distinguish this mechanism, and a multimodal head only at transition steps should repair it.
reason: The core mechanism (mode-averaging drift+multimodal-head repair) was published by WIMLE; the increment is only "shape discrimination+transition-step selectivity," and an error step alone is weak evidence because contact steps are intrinsically harder≠bimodal averaging, so it depends on the repair arm. Both halves have been published→novelty is capped, and no dimension reaches ≥7

idea: Most "action multimodality" in diffusion/flow-matching action heads may be task-irrelevant teleoperation jitter rather than strategic branching; replacing generative multimodality with a unimodal head+explicit discrete mode selection should match success on standard suites while saving K denoising steps, failing only on constructed true two-strategy tasks.
reason: Load-bearing-assumption break-glass condition ① fails the zero-headline-hit requirement (the core boundary claim is directly preoccupied by MARS Policy 2605.29766, overlap medium-high). Condition ② fails the ≥2 supporting results requirement (2510.26670 explicitly retains multimodality, and the conclusion reverses the idea's citation due to a misread; only 1 supports and 1 is partial). Under the ordinary form, novelty is substantially occupied.

idea: The trajectory-count/hour axis of VLA data scaling laws counts near-duplicate teleoperation demonstrations as new data, making effective data volume far smaller and the true scaling exponent steeper; existing marginal-return curves measure the wrong independent variable.
reason: The empirical premise—near-duplicate demonstrations in one environment have diminishing returns and diversity carries scaling—is occupied by 2410.18647 (overlap medium), capping novelty. However, the framing "reported exponent biased downward by redundancy+deduplication rate analytically predicts slope difference+random-subsample attribution" was not found, and the minimal falsification experiment is decisive and runnable on one GPU, though heavy, yielding borderline.

idea: Success rate on simulated manipulation leaderboards measures "skill given perfect state estimation": proprioception/force are unreported zero-noise ground truth in simulation; adding deployment-level sensor noise should systematically reorder the leaderboard, with degradation increasing with proprioceptive dependence.
reason: The empirical headline (inject proprioceptive noise→rankings reorder→degradation scales with proprioceptive dependence) is substantially covered by 2510.00037 (overlap medium-high), leaving only the "unreported confound" framing. The repair arm, "predict real-robot ranking," is untestable in an entirely simulated phase-1, and the residual cannot pass the novelty gate.

idea: Temporal context (frame stacks/action-history conditioning) may be a causal-confounding liability rather than load-bearing for contact manipulation; replace it with single-frame Markov+minimal gated memory, preserving performance with differences confined to a measurable few memory-critical steps.
reason: The headline (remove all history+single-frame gated memory+memory matters at only a few steps) is largely occupied by GMP 2604.18933, overlap=high, and its 27-42% gate activation rate contradicts "<15%". The difference is insufficient for clear-accept, and break-glass condition ① fails the zero-headline-hit requirement.

idea: The optimal VLA action-chunk length and reliable world-model rollout horizon may not be independent hyperparameters, but jointly determined by one measurable closed-loop action-conditioned state-decorrelation timescale τ(task), making separate tuning redundant.
reason: A single τ unifying both horizons is not occupied by one paper (overlap=medium in a crowded component space, with SA capped). The minimal falsification requires ≥8 tasks, training WM+VLA and jointly sweeping K*/H*; for one researcher on 1×H100 it is heavy, yielding 1 MAJOR.

idea: A VLA's "visual generalization" to new objects may primarily be prior interpolation from language instructions narrowing the action distribution rather than visual-feature generalization; replacing instructions with generic language should collapse novel-object success to base-rate while leaving in-distribution performance nearly unchanged.
reason: The 2×2 discriminating design is unoccupied, but LangForce 2601.15197 decomposes visual priors vs language posteriors and offers the opposite causal intuition. The estimand shifts between "language is necessary" and "generalization comes only from language," yielding 1 MAJOR and capping SA.

idea: Treat benchmark resolution itself as the estimand: bootstrap over seed×reset×episode to estimate the "rank-reversal probability" and minimum detectable effect between adjacent published methods. The proposition is that many published gaps lie below the benchmark noise floor, so single-score reporting measures noise.
reason: Prescreen direct hit: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.04233

idea: Proposition: a closed-loop VLA may not truly consume each observation; much of its action is motor-autoregressive autopilot. Define an "autopilot score" using the fraction of counterfactual action variance under frozen observations, test whether it predicts perturbation-recovery failure better than perception metrics, and reduce it through observation-forcing training; absent a signal, the "closed loop every step" assumption holds and the wager dies.
reason: The load-bearing-assumption form has a decisive falsification experiment and an observation-forcing repair arm, largely satisfying all four conditions; however, uncertain identifier self-check+the close SV-VLA (2604.02965) was not fully read, so zero headline hits are not verified to SA depth and novelty is capped.

idea: Replace binary "VLA follows language" success with measurable channel capacity: define and measure the phase-wise profile I(instruction;action|observation). The proposition is that this mutual information concentrates at task start and is near zero during execution, so start-only conditioning should match full-time conditioning within noise; existing benchmarks cannot distinguish continuous language control from a one-shot task selector.
reason: The MI estimator aligns with the headline, and the start-only-conditioning ablation can decisively falsify it; however, CAST (2508.13446) uses counterfactual replacement and already states the qualitative headline that "language concentrates at the start." With overlap=medium, novelty is capped, and this remains a pure diagnostic without a repair arm, so it stops at borderline.

idea: Name and measure "subgoal forgetting": long-horizon VLAs repeat completed subgoals. Define the redundant-retry rate and use a mechanism-separation arm to distinguish "effective context too short" from "missing explicit progress state." The proposition is that adding a completed-subgoal state token reduces retries more than simply extending history, making forgetting a representation gap rather than a context-length problem.
reason: The redundant-retry-rate estimand+the context-versus-representation separation arm are clean and falsifiable, but the remedy "explicit progress state fixes long-horizon repetition" is occupied by PALM/SADP/Notes-to-Self. With overlap=medium, novelty is capped; the increment is limited to the estimand and causal separation and cannot reach clear-accept.

idea: Selecting world models by one-step prediction error chooses the model that best fits the next frame rather than the one that best supports control; the rankings diverge across model classes, and replacing one-step error with a controllable-rollout divergence proxy should select a better controller.
reason: The headline is directly covered by RC-aux (2605.07278) (single-step error=a harmful load-bearing selection proxy, and replacing it with a control-relevant rollout metric selects a stronger controller), overlap=high and a direct hit. Assumption-removal channel condition ① fails the zero-headline-hit requirement, forcing Reject.

idea: Single-step forward throughput (FPS) in VLA inference benchmarks measures compute throughput rather than closed-loop controllable rate; under real perception+inference latency, action-chunk staleness is the binding variable, so FPS rankings should diverge from latency-aware closed-loop success rankings.
reason: The headline—that FPS measures compute throughput rather than latency-aware controllability and staleness is the load-bearing confound—is stated directly by RTC (2506.07339), overlap=high and a direct hit. A rank-divergence benchmark+staleness-corrected throughput is only a packaging increment, insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Cross-embodiment transfer gains are confounded by intrinsic target-embodiment ease: reported gains inflate as the target's from-scratch ceiling rises; difficulty normalization should reverse source-embodiment rankings, and selecting sources accordingly should improve target success.
reason: overlap=low with a proposition-level change in evaluation object and a difficulty-normalized source-selection repair arm (escaping the diagnostic borderline ceiling), but only 2 papers were fully read, and priorwork admits that specialized cross-embodiment evaluation work may be missing. The review depth cannot support SA-level novelty, capping it at AwR.

idea: Uniform full-frame resolution in VLA visual encoding may be load-bearing waste: manipulation attention concentrates near the end effector/contact region, so under equal FLOPs, proprioception-predicted foveated encoding should match uniform encoding if uniform resolution is not load-bearing.
reason: direct-hit: the headline mechanism (proprioception predicts the foreground for foveated, compute-aware VLA encoding) is fully covered by 2603.20668/2603.08124, overlap=high. The assumption-removal channel does not waive a direct hit; the equal-FLOPs attribution arm is insufficient for clear-accept, and the efficiency track is crowded.

idea: Unlabeled “reset/cleanup” actions in teleoperation corpora are learned as task actions, causing policies to spontaneously undo progress or release objects near success. Proposition: the share of reset segments predicts the terminal reversal rate, and filtering reset segments reduces terminal failures more than adding the same amount of data.
reason: overlap=low, with zero headline hits, an aligned estimand, and a decisive falsification experiment executable by one researcher on existing datasets. However, DQAF already notes reset/release behavior as a quality factor, leaving only the naming+falsifiable formulation rather than an oral-presentation-level contribution. Identifier checks also leave 3 papers uncertain, making the prior-work search thin and adding a MAJOR; cap at AwR.

idea: A VLA fails to follow negative or prohibitive instructions not because it cannot understand negation, but because negation abnormally amplifies attention to the forbidden object. Proposition: under “do not touch Y,” contact with Y exceeds the rate when Y is not mentioned at all, motivating a negation-aware repair.
reason: Mechanism transfer: ironic amplification is established in text (White Bear)/T2I (Pink Elephant). This idea transfers it across domains, but the three exception conditions, including nontrivial adaptation and a signal sufficient for clear-accept, are not all met → no SA. The directional VLA contact-rate measurement has zero hits, the estimand is aligned, and the experiment is executable → do not Reject. Identifier checks leave 4 papers uncertain, including a key precedent read only at abstract depth, adding a MAJOR and fixing the result at borderline AwR.

idea: Teleoperators unconsciously standardize initial object layouts into easy-to-grasp configurations, while benchmarks reuse the same distribution and inflate success rates. Name this the initial-state coverage gap. Proposition: success drops substantially under a natural initial-state distribution, and the drop is predicted by distance to the demonstration initial-state manifold.
reason: The headline—naming an initial-state coverage gap+an in-distribution-to-natural-distribution drop—is occupied by 2606.04233, and distance-based prediction is occupied by 2606.24403; both were opened and checked. Only the quantitative distance-ΔSR relationship+ranking reversal after correction+attribution arm remains unintegrated in one paper, which is insufficient for clear-accept→novelty is capped. The minimal falsification experiment is executable and decisive, so the verdict is AwR.

idea: Failure monitors are evaluated by detection accuracy, but their utility depends on whether the earliest detectable anomaly precedes the irreversible boundary. Define the signed actionable window=t_irreversible−t_detectable. Proposition: it is often negative in contact-rich or dynamic tasks, creating a fundamental blind window for reactive monitoring.
reason: The signed actionable window+“reactive blind window” conclusion has overlap=low and a decisive minimal falsification experiment. However, the identifier audit did not open every reported paper, the strongest counterexample 2606.05660 rendered incompletely, and the differentiation argument relies only on abstracts/API→novelty remains unverified. The thin prior-work search counts as 1 MAJOR finding, and the result is capped at AwR.

idea: Adjacent action chunks are independently generated from different observations, making the action trajectory discontinuous at their junction. Define a seam jump. Proposition: contact-phase failures and actuator jitter concentrate at chunk seams and are predicted by jump magnitude; boundary smoothing repairs them better than more frequent replanning.
reason: Direct hit (F1): 2603.11642, opened and checked, already defines the seam jump as second-order finite-difference jerk, shows failures concentrating at seams even in contact-free windows, and separates the effect from frequency. All three headline elements are occupied. The repair arm lies in dense Legato/LiPo/SEAM/RTC/REMAC blending literature, with no clear-accept-level difference.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that manufacturing and wear differences among nominally identical robot units are negligible and that one fleet checkpoint transfers directly across units; model unit variation as a required hidden domain.
reason: The load-bearing-assumption headline—treating unit variation as a hidden domain+fingerprint conditioning—is directly occupied by TAM (2606.06218). Both reported crack-evidence items were checked and found not to match: 2110.03239 is domain-randomization theory rather than a survey, and the openvla page provides no same-model unit variance. The break-glass route fails and the idea falls back to an ordinary form; the remaining “whether DR absorbs the variation” increment is, by the prior-work review’s own assessment, insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Negative transfer in a multitask VLA is not driven by task dissimilarity; it concentrates in task pairs with similar visual appearance but opposing force policies. Force-signature conflict predicts harmful task pairs better than visual similarity.
reason: No single paper occupies force-signature conflict as the main cause of negative transfer or its superiority to visual similarity (overlap low-medium). The minimal falsification experiment is executable and decisive, and the estimand is aligned. However, this is a pure competing explanation without a repair arm, and no dimension reaches 8+; under the rubric’s diagnostic ceiling, it is capped at borderline.

idea: The quality of human corrections decays within a supervision session as time on duty increases and vigilance declines. Late-session corrections are noisier labels, yet current methods pool the entire session with equal weight.
reason: Applying within-session vigilance decay as a source of correction-label noise+a downweighting decision is unoccupied and phase-1 executable. However, a synthetic supervisor whose quality decays with time builds in the result, while the headline estimand—real human within-session degradation—is not measured by the minimal falsification experiment. Extrapolation to the real setting requires additional data, capping the result at borderline.

idea: The gain from embodied chain-of-thought (embodied CoT) is not “reasoning,” but a side effect of longer conditioning context reducing action-distribution entropy; a length-matched semantically blank prefix should recover most of the gain.
reason: The falsifiable headline prediction—a length-matched semantically blank prefix should recover the gain—was already tested with the same control arm, length-matched thinking-tokens placeholders, in 2505.08243 and falsified: the blank prefix reduced performance by 3.8pp. Fine-grained causal ablations in 2603.12717 further show that semantic structure is load-bearing. Prior-work overlap=high is a direct hit; F1 is dominated-by-prior-work and therefore CRITICAL.

idea: The main reason contact-phase manipulation learns poorly is neither missing force supervision nor insufficient capacity; high-variance, high-magnitude contact-phase gradients are systematically suppressed by Adam second-moment normalization and gradient clipping, making the failure an optimization artifact.
reason: The minimal falsification package covers per-phase gradients, clipping-trigger rates, and second moments + repair arm b + a budget-matched force-supervision arm on 1×H100. It is executable and decisive, with overlap=low and a defended difference. However, the broader “BC failure=optimization artifact” framework is occupied by GVA (2310.11428), and the gain from the unconstrained repair is unverified. The clear-accept ceiling is not secure, so the result remains borderline.

idea: Long-horizon manipulation-planning failure is dominated by compounding forward-rollout drift. A backward-reachability world model, conditioned on a goal to predict predecessor states and actions, could bypass forward drift and make long-horizon planning substantially more stable at equal compute.
reason: The mechanism—using a backward generative predecessor world model to bypass forward drift in long-horizon manipulation planning—is covered by Time Reversal (1810.01128, manipulation)+Backward Learning (2312.05044, generative goal-conditioned predecessors). The difference is only packaging around a modern long-horizon benchmark+quantitative decay slope. The mechanism-transfer SA exception fails because the target domain already has hits→novelty is capped. Moreover, backward rollout accumulates its own reverse error, so the claim that it “fundamentally avoids forward-prediction error accumulation” is only partly true and the advantage is not guaranteed.

idea: One untested cause of early saturation in noise-space RL steering for a frozen VLA is reward hacking of a VLM-judge in latent noise space: the policy finds noise that makes the judge report success without completing the task. An oracle-reward control separates the explanations, and judge ensembles or force-signature cross-checks provide repair arms.
reason: The mechanism—policy exploitation of VLM-judge false positives, with reward rising while true success does not—is highly homologous to Dark Side (2409.15922), giving overlap=medium→novelty is capped. The oracle control is a decisive kill experiment and the estimand, divergence between judge false-positive rate and held-out true success, is aligned, so the result is AwR. The load-bearing-removal SA route fails because ① overlap≠low and ② only 1 crack item matches (InfoRM matches/LRM is partial); no SA.

idea: Cross-embodiment transfer failures concentrate near kinematic singularities and joint-limit regions unique to the target embodiment, which the source never encountered; generic embodiment distance does not predict them. Failure is predicted by the fraction of a trajectory traversing target singular neighborhoods, and singularity-aware retargeting repairs it without more data.
reason: Each leg of the singularity/joint-limit predictor has strong precedent: FeasibleCap/RichMap predictors, the CEI distance view, and singularity-aware IK repair. With overlap=medium, only integrated naming+controlled diagnostic remain open→novelty is capped, and the mechanism-transfer SA exception fails because the target domain has nonzero hits. The controlled regression is decisive and the no-new-data repair arm is executable→AwR.

idea: After human takeover, control is returned from a recovered configuration left by the operator, but that configuration lies outside the policy’s demonstration distribution. Proposition: post-handoff failure exceeds cold-start failure, with the gap predicted by distance to the demonstration manifold—an intervention can help locally yet harm at handoff.
reason: RaC (2509.07953) already covers the handoff-state mixture-distribution phenomenon+a rewind-before-handoff repair. The idea adds only measurement+naming+estimand formalization, with weak independence and overlap=medium→novelty is capped. An experiment isolating excess handoff failure without label noise is decisive and executable→barely AwR, the weakest of the three.

idea: The load-bearing cause of long-horizon drift in a learned world model is not the character of one-step error, but autoregression itself. Replacing rollout with joint denoising of an entire trajectory should change error growth from compounding with horizon to nearly flat at matched capacity and compute.
reason: This has a load-bearing-assumption form and a cheap, decisive falsification experiment; the unverified wager is not counted as a MAJOR. However, the headline has overlap=medium, and joint-denoise WM alternatives to AR are already widely implemented by Diffuser/DWM/Diffusion Forcing. The matched-capacity attribution is not a clear-accept-level difference, and break-glass condition ①, zero headline hits, fails→novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: Diffusion and flow-matching action heads have an intrachunk mode-incoherence failure independent of interchunk seams: during denoising, a single chunk switches from one multimodal mode to another. Mode-anchored sampling should repair it better than seam smoothing.
reason: The seam-separated diagnostic+mode-anchored sampling admits a decisive falsification experiment, but PF-DAG (2602.21684) substantially occupies both the mechanism and repair. The only surviving novelty is the thin diagnostic distinction from seams→overlap=medium and novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: Teleoperation data are collected in sessions, and operator warm-up and fatigue make demonstration quality vary systematically with within-session time. Reweighting by session position should improve performance more than adding the same amount of unweighted demo data.
reason: Within-session time as a quality axis+index reweighting vs adding the same amount of raw demo data define a decisive falsification experiment with a shuffle control. However, the causal account is occupied from both DQAF and human-factors directions, and it depends on session boundaries that DROID does not explicitly confirm and must be clustered from collector ID+timestamp→overlap=medium and novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: VLA inference-efficiency leaderboards measure action-chunk length rather than deployment responsiveness. Rankings reverse when methods are reevaluated at equal reaction frequency; single-step latency and throughput are mismeasures confounded by chunking.
reason: No single paper directly covers the measurement headline (overlap=medium), but the repair arm—a responsiveness-normalized metric+adaptive chunk scheduling—is densely occupied by AAC/DVAC/VLA-Corrector. The difference is insufficient for clear-accept, so novelty is capped at borderline.

idea: Success rates on contact-intensive manipulation benchmarks are dominated by failure variance in the grasp-and-approach phase, so they measure grasp reliability rather than contact skill. Resetting directly into the contact state isolates the latter and reorders policy rankings.
reason: GraspCorrect already establishes the core empirical fact in one paper: pre-contact grasping dominates SR+an oracle start from a successful grasp isolates the remainder. Novelty is therefore heavily eroded, while “share of variance across policies”≠“what the benchmark measures,” leaving an estimand-alignment concern. Cap at AwR.

idea: FVD and pixel metrics for world models measure passive-dynamics fidelity, while control value exists only in the action-controllable variance subspace. The controllable-variance fraction is an overlooked confounder: as it shrinks, FVD and downstream return become increasingly decoupled.
reason: Half of the headline—the decoupling of FVD/pixel metrics from control—is already covered by VP²/WorldArena/2606.07687. Only the estimand “controllable-variance fraction as a reportable moderator of the decoupling” is new. With overlap=medium and an insufficient clear-accept-level difference, cap at borderline.

idea: Proposition: mobile-manipulation policies trained with base and arm as one synchronous action space fail where slow base settling injects a moving reference into precise arm tracking, rather than because the task is difficult. Base–arm bandwidth conflict predicts failure better than task difficulty, and gating arm refinement on base stability provides a targeted repair.
reason: The core causal account—base motion disrupting precise arm tracking as the main failure source—is acknowledged by the target, Mobile ALOHA, and independently named or formalized by EMMa/ABot. The gated repair≈the known Stop-and-Grasp method (overlap=high). Only the estimand “bandwidth conflict as a failure predictor independent of difficulty” remains unformalized, which is insufficient to support the difference.

idea: Proposition: the advantage of perceptive over blind legged locomotion is usually attributed to terrain preview, but vision may primarily re-anchor state estimation by correcting proprioceptive drift caused by slip and deformation. Ablations that shorten the preview horizon and give blind locomotion oracle drift-free proprioception separate the mechanisms. The predicted advantage comes from re-anchoring on slippery or soft terrain and from preview only on geometrically complex terrain.
reason: overlap=low, and the reverse attribution between preview and re-anchoring+the terrain-family×ablation-arm separation is supported by a strong search (6 papers read, no uncertain identifiers). However, the form is purely diagnostic; the two arms are discriminating ablations rather than repair arms whose implementation itself improves performance. No dimension reaches 8+, and there is no strong prior for a surprising finding, so cap at borderline.

idea: Proposition: a VLA interprets spatial language such as “behind X” or “left of X” in an egocentric camera frame by default, and failures arise from reference-frame mismatch rather than weak grounding. The success gap between egocentrically consistent and allocentric instructions separates the explanations; a reference-frame conditioning token should repair allocentric failures better than more language data.
reason: The core causal account—mismatch between the default egocentric frame and the required allocentric/object-centric frame, rather than weak grounding—and the reference-frame conditioning repair are already published across several VLM-QA papers (2602.05789/2410.17385/2602.15892, overlap medium-high). Migration to VLA+an in-policy frame token+frame-angle regression remains, but the difference is insufficient for clear-accept. The mechanism-transfer SA exception fails because the target domain has nonzero hits in SpatialVLA/OC-VLA and the adaptation is fairly direct; cap at borderline.

idea: The assumption that the action flow head of a frozen VLA must cold-start from data-independent Gaussian noise is not load-bearing. Warm-starting the ODE from the end of the previous action chunk can remove chunk-seam discontinuity, making cold start rather than model error or chunk stitching the root cause.
reason: Direct hit: WarmPrior (2605.13959) already delivers the full headline: remove the Gaussian cold start+use a historical-action warm source to repair seams+reduce steps. ActionCache/Legato cover two additional corners. With overlap=high, exception route ① fails because there is not zero headline occupancy.

idea: Real-time latency for edge-deployed VLA systems is dominated by memory bandwidth from long-context KV and repeated action-head forward passes rather than policy FLOPs; fewer denoising steps, early exit, and longer chunks optimize secondary terms.
reason: The direct hit, “direct-hit:2603.02271,” already performs the edge roofline decomposition on Jetson Orin/Thor, attributes ~75% to memory-bound execution, and finds that a 5× compute increase delivers only a 1.4× speedup. VLA-Perf/EfficientVLA provide further support. With overlap=high, the headline is occupied.

idea: Language instructions in robot datasets are often inferred retrospectively by annotators watching trajectories and systematically differ from the executor’s actual intent. This retrospective-labeling noise contributes to instruction-following failure.
reason: There is no direct hit at overlap=medium, and “mismatch rate between inferred annotations and execution intent” is genuinely unoccupied as a named, measured label-noise source. However, the proposition-style diagnostic is ceiling-limited+the difference is below clear-accept+the estimand is confounded because high-mismatch segments may simply be harder; random filtering does not separate difficulty→cap at AwR.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that every VLA action step must condition on visual appearance features. Feed motion and geometry instead, using optical flow+depth, and test whether appearance is load-bearing outside the few steps requiring semantic recognition.
reason: The idea has a load-bearing-assumption form, but break-glass does not apply: overlap=medium with nonzero hits, and the two crack-evidence items are one “partial” and one “mismatch” caused by a same-name miscitation. The ordinary-form minimal falsification experiment is cheap and decisive, with no direct-hit, so the result is AwR rather than reject. VO-DP already shows through task-level ablations that appearance and geometry are both load-bearing; novelty is capped and no SA is given.

idea: Proposition: policies with equal success rates differ systematically in the minimum recoverability margin along successful trajectories. Current leaderboards measure only the endpoint and reward lucky traversal near irreversible boundaries. The minimum trajectory margin predicts failure under perturbation better than success rate and supports policy selection.
reason: A changed evaluation target+margin-based selection repair escapes the diagnostic ceiling; the estimand matches the headline, and the experiment is decisive and executable on one GPU. However, the core estimand “continuous margin to failure→robustness prediction” already appears in Energy Margin/HJ reachability (overlap=medium). The mechanism-transfer exception fails because the target domain has nonzero hits, so novelty is capped at borderline.

idea: Proposition: VLA failures on quantities and quantifiers are not visual counting errors, but missing “remaining items to manipulate” progress state in the action head. A remaining-count token should repair performance better than full visual grounding of all targets.
reason: The named problem+a remaining-count head provide a repair arm, with overlap=low, clean dual oracle arms separating perception from action, and a decisive single-GPU experiment. It is the strongest of the three. However, the nearest neighbor 2602.24143 gives the opposite attribution—failure on the grounding side—and requires direct confrontation, while the benchmark is narrow, using synthetic LIBERO with N≤4 targets. Only Higher reaches 8 while remaining below the “high-probability clear-accept + oral-presentation” SA ceiling.

idea: Action chunking is not load-bearing for policy quality; it is load-bearing only for inference-latency compensation. With zero-latency asynchronous execution, chunk=1+ history conditioning should match the optimal chunk across all tasks, including non-Markovian ones.
reason: This removes a load-bearing assumption and has a cheap, decisive minimal falsification experiment under a real forcing constraint. However, the “quality vs latency” causal-account framework is already formalized by 2507.09061, with the opposite conclusion+a markovian lower bound. Novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: Stepwise autoregressive rollout is not load-bearing for a world model; compounding error is its structural cost rather than an essential property. A horizon-conditioned direct skip predictor can avoid accumulation between steps.
reason: overlap=high: the causal account in which direct skip or horizon-conditioned predictors structurally remove compounding error is collectively occupied by TDM/γ-models/ADM/Jumpy World Models (2602.19634), covering both mechanism and naming. This is a direct-hit.

idea: Language conditioning is not load-bearing for deployment-level manipulation generalization in a VLA; it serves only as a task-id. Replace language with goal-image conditioning, preserve compositional generalization, and conclude that efforts to “restore language grounding” target the wrong object.
reason: This removes a load-bearing assumption and offers a decisive replacement arm+placebo test; eliminating VLM compute is a real forcing constraint. However, the goal-vs-language space is heavily occupied and several papers provide contrary evidence that language is necessary for unseen generalization. With overlap=medium, novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: The main cause of long-horizon world-model rollout drift is decaying action conditioning—the model progressively ignores actions—rather than compounded pixel error. The decay curve should predict downstream planning failure better than prediction error.
reason: The headline, “drift is driven by collapse of action conditioning+repairable,” is occupied by Delta-JEPA (2606.31232)+Persistent-WM (2603.25685). Only the diagnostic increment of using decay as a stepwise failure predictor remains; the lowest verdict across three reviews is reject.

idea: A discrete action tokenizer outperforms a continuous head because quantization removes subthreshold action noise, not because of a sequence-modeling prior. Giving the continuous head a matched quantization noise floor should close the gap.
reason: The competing account+noise-floor discrimination experiment is relatively new, but ActionCodec (2602.15397) occupies adjacent tokenizer resistance to overfitting and 2412.16326 is an isomorphic precedent in vision, capping novelty at medium. One estimand-alignment issue adds a MAJOR.

idea: Define decision-relevant action bandwidth: frequency content in teleoperation actions in the >10 Hz band is interpolation or hand-tremor noise that models fit and evaluations reward. Training and evaluation should be filtered by frequency band.
reason: FreqPolicy (2506.01583) directly occupies and exploits low-frequency action compressibility. The estimand that evaluation rewards high-frequency noise is weak and the increment is thin; the lowest verdict across three reviews is reject.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that a VLA must re-encode vision from raw pixels at every control step. Vision is load-bearing only at sparse perceptual decision points; a learned trigger determines when to re-encode, while other steps reuse features+proprioception.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: AC2-VLA (2601.19634)/VLA-Cache/DySL-VLA (2602.22896) already cover adaptive or cached visual computation and redundancy in an always-on backbone. All five axiom-removal fields are present, but the headline is occupied.

idea: OOD failure in manipulation policies is predicted earlier and more accurately by novelty in proprioceptive or action space than by visual OOD detection; replace the visual gate with a proprioceptive-space conformal gate.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: ActProbe (2606.08508) claims that action signals predict failure before it becomes visible, so one paper covers the headline.

idea: Define intrinsic policy reliability=the success variance induced by action sampling from a fixed initial state. It predicts deployment degradation better than mean success rate, and low-variance decoding improves worst-case performance.
reason: Fixed-state variance is already formalized by PhAIL (2605.29710)/Rethink-Repeatable (2505.08216). The idea is ceiling-limited as a pure diagnostic, and it was excluded by the deep-review quota.

idea: The real function of web vision-language co-training for a VLA is not preserving semantic knowledge, but preventing the visual encoder from collapsing onto a narrow robot distribution. Encoder-rank regularization can replace it cheaply using robot-only data.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: Preserving-Pretrained-Representations (2509.11417, frozen or partially frozen encoders prevent collapse)+the established role of mainstream co-training in preventing overfitting already cover the claim, and inexpensive replacements also exist.

idea: Negative transfer in a multitask VLA is caused not by shared-representation capacity conflict, but by opposing contact-force gradients at visually neighboring states.
reason: A local repair is valuable and falsifiable, but the current controls cannot separate opposing contact-force gradients from generally difficult states, and the full experiment matrix has not been shown feasible on 1×H100 within 5 days.

idea: The manipulation gain from high-resolution tactile sensing comes not from spatial texture content, but from a phase clock supplied by contact transitions.
reason: Replacing tactile imaging with an event clock is sufficiently distinct to warrant testing, but the strongest-baseline set omits recent phase-aware methods, while the training scale is ambiguous and doubtful under a budget of 1×H100 for 3 days.

idea: The emergence breakpoint for compositional skills is caused not by parameter scale crossing a threshold, but by the training-data skill-transfer graph crossing a connectivity threshold.
reason: The priorwork review found high overlap with direct work showing that a connectivity phase transition in the training-data support graph determines and moves the capability-emergence breakpoint. A robot skill graph and λ2 are only target-domain instantiations and cannot support clear-accept-level novelty.

idea: VLA failures on objects with repeated appearance arise mainly from identity rebinding after occlusion rather than single-frame spatial grounding. Having the action head reference persistent cross-time object identities instead of current-frame detection slots should improve only identity-swap conditions.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06481

idea: The fixed-budget bottleneck in an object-centric world model is not token count, but continued computation in regions where physical constraints have already closed. Distill a routing policy from an oracle based on physical-residual propagation, then spend the saved compute on a longer horizon to improve planning over strong learned-router baselines.
reason: A longer horizon under a fixed budget has clear efficiency and planning value, and the minimal falsification experiment is executable. However, target-domain dynamic world-model routing has a direct neighbor, and object-level routing by physical residual is not yet a clear-accept-level difference.

idea: Long-horizon world-model planning fails not because one-step prediction error is large, but because latent state incorrectly merges histories with identical futures under the behavior policy that diverge under changed actions. Explicitly penalizing counterfactual branch collapse should improve planning more than further reducing reconstruction error.
reason: UWM-JEPA, causal-state/PSR, and Counterfactual Regularization collectively occupy the headline estimand and training mechanism. The remaining history-pairing metric and loss cannot clear the F1 novelty CRITICAL.

idea: Long-tail latency in an edge VLA is not exogenous noise; it can form a closed-loop thermal instability through “high-frequency corrective actions→chip heating→frequency throttling→staler observations→more corrections.” Jointly scheduling computation depth and action cadence by thermal headroom should be more stable than allocating compute by sample difficulty.
reason: The thermal–latency–correction loop has clear deployment value and an executable experiment, but the gap in industrial-occupancy search and insufficient causal identification in the current experiment each add one MAJOR, capping the result at accept-w-rev.

idea: Failure across control frequencies is caused not by temporal aliasing, but by action tokens treating “amount per step” as a physical invariant. Representing duration-aware integrated displacement or impulse should preserve the same physical effect at unseen frequencies.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.24049

idea: Filtering demonstrations by success rate and action smoothness systematically removes high-curvature but successful recovery segments. Cleaner data therefore have less recovery-state coverage; retaining samples by their ability to return to the task manifold after a deviation should improve perturbed recovery.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.15587

idea: If a safety filter records only the corrected executed action, it disguises a “dangerous proposal→safe outcome” as a safe demonstration. Recording both the policy proposal and filter execution, then training with intervention awareness, should reduce the raw dangerous-proposal rate instead of merely hiding it behind a shield.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09778

idea: During grasping, independent persistent slots in an object-centric world model are not stable Markov units. Temporarily merge contact-connected robot and object slots into a dynamic compound, then split them when contact ends, to eliminate prediction jumps at grasp boundaries.
reason: The rollout/MPC result does not establish the Markov property, and the minimal experiment omits ContactNets and existing slot-merge nearest-neighbor baselines.

idea: The VLA gain from action-history conditioning comes mainly from leakage of demonstrator intent rather than better state estimation. Replacing action history with its observed physical effects should prevent self-locking onto an erroneous branch at deployment.
reason: CRITICAL F1: PALR, copycat, and latching directly occupy the headline that past expert actions leak information and cause deployment-time self-locking. The effect-history comparison and swap experiment are only narrower repair differences, insufficient for clear accept.

idea: Overoptimism in world-model planning is caused not by a small ensemble, but by bootstrap members sharing the same blind spots in behavior data. Partitioning members by intervention family should restore disagreement on counterfactual actions actively selected by the planner and block model exploitation.
reason: No single paper directly occupies the mechanism chain, and OOD robustness has clear-accept potential. However, the nearest strong baseline is omitted and the current AUROC/success-rate design cannot identify the causal claim of a “shared blind spot”; two MAJOR findings cap the result.

idea: Current “joules per success” metrics measure the mechanical work required to complete the task rather than energy wasted by the policy and computing system; payload and lifting height alone can fabricate algorithmic efficiency rankings.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.06295

idea: Current long-horizon success rates measure multiplicative exposure to the number of events that can fail, not reasoning over cross-step dependencies. Orthogonalizing exposure count and dependency depth should reveal the true gain from memory mechanisms only on the latter axis.
reason: LongBench and ATOM-Bench already occupy the headline of separating execution exposure from historical dependency at high overlap. Moreover, the invalid N=2, D=4 cell and inconsistent arithmetic for 3,200 episodes make the minimal falsification experiment currently infeasible.

idea: Current robot-data diversity scores measure state-appearance coverage, not coverage of identifiable counterfactual action effects at the same local state. Datasets with identical appearance coverage can therefore have entirely different value for learning controllability.
reason: Local action causal effects and offline data filtering have strong nearest neighbors, while observational transitions alone cannot identify the claimed rank of counterfactual interventions. Adding controlled interventions could still yield a useful data-audit method.

idea: Action variance in diffusion policies and ensemble disagreement measure the number of valid policy branches, not failure risk. Existing uncertainty metrics therefore flag as dangerous states with many distinct actions that all succeed.
reason: Diff-DAgger, FIPER, and related work directly occupy “multimodal action disagreement does not equal failure risk.” Without task-conditioned outcome prediction and failure calibration, the effect quotient cannot independently estimate risk.

idea: It is not load-bearing to concatenate asynchronous RGB, tactile, and proprioceptive streams from the same VLA control step into a “simultaneous snapshot.” Under different sensor rates and clock drift, a continuous-time event stream carrying raw timestamps should approximate the truly observable state better than an approximately synchronized tuple.
reason: CRITICAL: InFuser directly covers the headline chain “asynchronous multirate sensor streams→continuous latent state→control at arbitrary times.” Language conditioning, modality extension, and the skew protocol are insufficient to recover clear-accept-level novelty.

idea: Exploitation by a visual world-model planner may not fabricate success from nothing; it may actively hide failure behind the robot arm or an object so that its own reward model cannot see it. Visibility-counterfactual consistency should remove inflated value only from self-occluding trajectories.
reason: The headline of active occlusion deception is empirically occupied by SOLE-R1, and the current baselines omit the most relevant hard-negative and reward-robustification paths from priorwork. The visibility-counterfactual repair retains borderline methodological value.

idea: A memory-augmented VLA can enter cascading failure after a safety shield intervenes because internal memory updates along the unexecuted proposal while the real environment advances under the corrected action. Feeding back executed action and shield delta should provide a targeted repair only in the post-intervention window.
reason: The divergence between selected and executed actions is covered by Modified-Action MDP and Recovery RL, while specialization to a recurrent VLA has not yet shown a nontrivial transfer. The current minimal experiment also fails to isolate memory mismatch from dangerous-state selection as the causal estimand.

idea: Define “self-induced observability loss”: robot actions first occlude, blur, or saturate their own sensors and then change task state. Measure the reduction in future-state identifiability caused by an action and use it as a planning cost.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.12557

idea: Define “endogenous modality availability” for tactile policies: tactile evidence exists only after the policy actively establishes informative contact. Measure the probability of acquiring evidence and its conditional decision value after acquisition, avoiding the false conclusion that touch is useless when the policy never probes.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.00215

idea: An alternative explanation for long-horizon failures at subtask boundaries is that chunk sampling/padding undertrains boundary states. After separating this effect from inference-noise attribution, supervision-coverage density should still predict the failure map, and boundary resampling should improve only those states.
reason: The competing boundary-coverage explanation+two-arm falsification experiment is executable; priorwork gives overlap=medium vs 2603.11642, while the difference does not reach a clear-accept ceiling, so cap at AwR.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that VLA training and evaluation must lock automatic exposure, white balance, and the ISP as static calibration. Enabling field AE should reorder rankings, while AE-aware augmentation should repair photometric-drift failures better than a larger backbone.
reason: Removing the locked ISP has ≥2 matching cracks and a falsification experiment that can kill the wager. However, overlap=medium blocks break-glass SA, and enabling “AE” in LIBERO/Simpler is estimand-misaligned with the closed-loop AE of a commodity camera.

idea: Define “mid-episode goal interruptibility”: completion rate and latency after rewriting the instruction during an episode. Existing VLAs optimize a single-goal closed loop and systematically fail after a goal switch because of action-commitment inertia; switch-conditioned replanning should improve only interruptible tasks.
reason: CRITICAL F1: SwitchVLA (2506.03574) is an isomorphic occupant of the mid-episode instruction-change estimand/protocol. Commitment inertia+history clearing is insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Evolution: recompute only physical-residual-frontier tokens in an object-centric world model, add strong Mixture-of-Depths/learned-router baselines, and force saved compute into a longer planning horizon. Change the signal from merely 35% compute savings to long-horizon success at equal FLOPs.
reason: The priorwork review has overlap=high, and DDP-WM occupies the headline through sparse primary recomputation+MPC. The residual=an explicit residual criterion+MoD/horizon protocol that does not reach a clear-accept-level difference. The four-arm falsification omits a real DDP-WM baseline (F1+F3).

idea: Remove the assumption that a BC data pipeline must first filter for success while discarding failure and recovery trajectories or training a separate recovery policy. Train one policy with weighted mixed trajectories; perturbed recovery should improve without reducing clean success.
reason: The priorwork review has overlap=high: ReTVL/SSDF are nearly isomorphic “mixed/imperfect weighted single-policy BC” methods. The axiom-removal route fails twice: ① overlap≠low and ② only 1 crack fully matches, with RACER partial. The remainder=an axiom falsification protocol insufficient for clear-accept (F1+ axiom-removal framing downgrade).

idea: A world model couples exogenous scene evolution and intervenable causal effects in one prediction head and loss. Split them into two heads and impose action-permutation consistency on the intervenable head; planning success should rise while total prediction MSE remains unchanged, showing that total error is not the planning bottleneck.
reason: Iso-Dream/VDFD already occupy “separate controllable and uncontrollable heads+emphasize the controllable side in planning.” Action-permutation consistency and the proposition “MSE↑/flat while planning improves” add mechanism detail but not enough for SA. The falsification omits a real Iso-Dream baseline (F1+F3); after that repair, the idea remains borderline.

idea: A large share of cross-embodiment transfer failure comes not from kinematic differences, but from timescale differences: the target embodiment cannot mechanically track the action bandwidth achievable by the source.
reason: overlap=medium; UMI-on-Air already occupies the account that dynamics mismatch is the primary cause. The bandwidth ratio+source-side low-pass filter provide a falsifiable narrowing, but not a clear-accept-level difference sufficient for SA. This yields 1×MAJOR F1 overall.

idea: A multicamera manipulation policy is not load-bearing on extrinsic calibration into a shared world frame. Remove calibration and use view-independent encoding+relative-geometry bootstrapping; under camera perturbations, it should match the success rate of a calibrated system.
reason: CRITICAL F1: priorwork has overlap=high. MV-MWM already demonstrates uncalibrated multiview closed-loop control on a real robot, and CamVLA demonstrates calib-free closed-loop control. The headline “precalibrated fusion can be removed” is already weakened, while only 1 crack matches.

idea: Failure under underspecified language instructions is caused not by grounding error, but by regression averaging across multiple valid target modes into “stop in the middle.” A target-set coverage loss should repair underspecified instructions better than higher visual resolution.
reason: The causal-separation proposition is viable but has MAJOR×2: F1, medium overlap with IBC/IntentVLA/DP; F3, the minimal falsification uses “standard VLA BC” as the strongest baseline and omits DP or another multimodal-head control, making the signal easy to confound.

idea: Current VLA leaderboards report mean success over a fixed N episodes. At N≤20, gaps between adjacent methods often lie within binomial noise; sequential testing or optional stopping should reduce false ranking under the same total rollout budget.
reason: Prescreen direct occupancy: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.13616

idea: Recheck: the utility of a failure monitor depends on the signed actionable window t_irreversible−t_detectable, which may be negative in contact-rich or dynamic tasks and create a fundamental blind window for reactive monitoring.
reason: Naming the signed actionable window Δ=t_irr−t_det and proposing that it is often negative in contact tasks are valid. The priorwork review has overlap=medium and an estimand difference from VLA-FAIL, whose AUCPDT is anchored to the episode endpoint. However, this is a pure diagnostic/probe without a repair arm, no dimension reaches 8+, and the difference is not established at clear-accept level → novelty is capped at borderline; no SA.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that effective manipulation demonstrations must be generated by real-time closed-loop teleoperation on the target robot. If an equal amount of human-only offline demonstration, retargeted+calibrated with very little robot data, matches pure real-time teleoperation BC, real-time in-loop demonstration is not load-bearing.
reason: CRITICAL F1/direct-hit: HumanEgo (2605.24934) directly demonstrates the headline under matched collection time: human video outperforms teleoperated ACT by 41%; it also argues that the budget should not be spent on teleoperation rigs. The priorwork review has overlap=high, and although three crack items match, break-glass① fails because headline occupancy is nonzero. The controlled difference of ≤10% calibration+the same BC head is insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that a VLA visual tower must be initialized with internet image-text contrastive weights from the CLIP/SigLIP family. Under matched architecture and robot-data budget, a robot-video SSL or from-scratch visual tower that matches SigLIP initialization would show that web contrastive initialization is not load-bearing.
reason: CRITICAL/F1: R3M/MVP/ACT already weaken the claim that CLIP/SigLIP image-text initialization is required. With overlap=high, a matched-budget three-arm VLA study is a nearby transfer ablation rather than a clear-accept-level difference.

idea: Remove the assumption that standard manipulation-policy evaluation must use independently reset i.i.d. episodes as its atomic unit. Continuous long-session evaluation without reset should reverse rankings relative to i.i.d. evaluation, showing that resets hide false generalization dependent on human cleanup.
reason: Both exception conditions for the load-bearing-assumption form fail: overlap=medium and only 1/2 cracks match. The headline experiment is separable from EARL/AutoEval, but novelty is capped and cannot receive SA. The falsification is executable and has no CRITICAL finding.

idea: Planning degradation in world models is attributed to pixel/latent drift, but an alternative is that the planner’s effective exploration radius in action dimensions shrinks exponentially with horizon. At equal reconstruction error, increasing action-side temperature should improve planning more than further reducing reconstruction error.
reason: MAJOR: the estimand is misaligned—increasing action temperature≠showing that conditional action entropy shrinks exponentially with horizon. MAJOR: the difference from the “reconstruction≠plannability” occupancy in World-in-World/RC-aux is insufficient for clear-accept.

idea: Human pairwise preferences label the recoverability margin near the annotation moment rather than global trajectory quality. A local-window preference model should predict perturbed failure better than trajectory-level preference and improve weighted BC only in high-risk phases.
reason: MAJOR: the headline “human labels=recoverability margin” is misaligned with the signal “local labels better predict irreversible failure under perturbation.” Preference Transformer and related work occupy the neighborhood → novelty is capped; no SA.

idea: Remove the load-bearing assumption that ablation and model selection must rely on a privileged environment success detector as the sole ground truth. If rankings under onboard-observable success estimates reverse relative to an oracle, oracle-ground-truth evaluation is not load-bearing and paper conclusions do not transfer to deployable observations.
reason: The load-bearing SA route fails because overlap=medium+only 1 crack matches. AutoEval/SIMPLER already occupy ranking consistency, while a reversal can be confounded with a poor onboard detector (estimand MAJOR).
